Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

FAA Presses California for Records in Airport-Funding Dispute

California airport funding dispute draws new FAA scrutiny.

The FAA's records request signals potential federal intervention in state airport financing decisions, which could establish precedent for future regulatory oversight of local aviation infrastructure funding.

Diversos eventos previos al lanzamiento, del lanzamiento y de la misión Artemis II de la NASA alrededor de la Luna se transmitirán en línea. La agencia tiene como fecha objetivo no antes del miércoles 1 de abril para este vuelo de prueba, con una ventana de lanzamiento de dos horas que se abre a...

NASA's Artemis II crewed lunar mission targets April 1 launch with comprehensive online coverage strategy to engage global audiences in this critical Moon exploration milestone.

DJI Avata 360 Sets New Standards for Immersive 360 FPV Flying

Equipped with DJI’s flagship 1-inch imaging, O4+ video transmission, and omnidirectional obstacle sensing, creators can now...

DJI's 1-inch sensor and omnidirectional obstacle detection in consumer FPV drones significantly reduce collision risks while enabling professional-grade immersive content creation at mass-market price points.

Southwest Airlines Transforms Austin-Bergstrom Airport, Unleashing A New Era For Travelers And Crew

The carrier has scheduled almost 3,500 departures from the Texan facility this month alone.

Southwest's massive departure surge in Austin signals aggressive market expansion in a competitive Texas market, likely driven by its largest focus city strategy outside of traditional hubs.

Pentagon eyes redirecting Ukraine’s Patriot missiles to Middle East

The Pentagon is considering whether to redirect air defense interceptor missiles originally earmarked for Ukraine to the Middle…...

U.S. military supply chain constraints may force strategic allocation decisions that pit European security commitments against Middle Eastern operational demands.

Virgin Australia named world’s best cabin crew for eighth year running

Virgin Australia has been named World’s Best Cabin Crew for the eighth consecutive year in the AirlineRatings.com World’s…...

Sustained cabin crew excellence represents a critical competitive differentiator as airlines compete on service quality amid post-pandemic staffing challenges.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport Remains North America's Busiest By Total Passengers

With over 100 million passengers handled in 2025, ATL continues to dominate global rankings for the world's busiest airport.

ATL's 100-million-passenger milestone reflects sustained hub consolidation, requiring airlines to optimize connectivity and ground operations to maintain competitive advantages.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective risk controls require organizations to document why residual risks are acceptable, creating accountability that transforms theoretical safety analysis into measurable operational outcomes.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s Hubble Detects First-Ever Spin Reversal of Tiny Comet

Astronomers using NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope have found evidence that the spinning of a small comet slowed and then reversed its direction of rotation, offering a dramatic example of how volatile activity can affect the spin and physical evolution of small bodies in the solar system. This is the...

Hubble's discovery that cometary spin can reverse demonstrates how outgassing forces fundamentally reshape small celestial bodies, providing critical data for spacecraft navigation and mission planning near asteroids and comets.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Hughes targets sovereign satcom demand with network control software

Company rolls out AI-enabled modem as militaries shift to hybrid networks...

Hughes' AI-enabled modem addresses military demands for seamless integration across satellite and terrestrial networks, positioning the company to capture growing sovereign satcom modernization budgets.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Video Shows Russian Geran Drone Shot Down by Rifle-Armed Ukrainian Yak-52 Crew

A first-person point-of-view video shows the crew member in the rear seat of a Ukrainian Yak-52 shooting with a rifle at a Russian Geran drone, which later detonates in a powerful explosion. A video that emerged on Mar. 24, 2026, shows a Russian Geran-type One-Way Attack (OWA) drone exploding...

Ukrainian pilots are adapting vintage Yak-52 trainers as low-cost defensive platforms against drone threats, demonstrating resourceful air defense alternatives when modern systems are unavailable.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 26, 2026

$147,000 Damage: Military KC-46A Tanker Rips Up 25 Foot Section Of Airport Tarmac

The continuous high temperature and pressure from the engine blast affected the integrity of the parking ramp, resulting in this damage.

Engine exhaust erosion reveals critical infrastructure vulnerability for large military aircraft, raising maintenance costs and operational planning requirements at shared-use facilities.

See Simple Flying →

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures reflect how regional airports must balance operational efficiency with community relations in congested metropolitan airspace.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Here’s How Much Larger The Boeing 777-300ER’s Windows Are Compared To The Airbus A350

Did you know that the older Triple-Seven has larger windows than a 21st-century clean-sheet carbon-composite widebody?

The 777-300ER's larger windows provide competitive cabin ambiance advantages despite the A350's newer design, influencing passenger experience and airline differentiation strategies.

See Simple Flying →

The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) has issued a final demand letter to AirAsia Philippines for…...

Regulatory non-compliance creates existential risk for airlines, as licensing suspension could force operational shutdown and strand thousands of passengers.

Volotea closes smaller than anticipated funding round following strong results

Regional low-cost carrier Volotea has completed its latest capital increase round. The airline announced in September 2024 that…...

Volotea's smaller-than-planned funding round despite strong operational results suggests investor caution about regional LCC expansion despite carrier performance improvements.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a leading helicopter fatality cause because pilots systematically underestimate hazard proximity and overestimate avoidance reaction time in low-altitude operations.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Airline That Introduced Lie-Flat Business Class Seats To The World

Find out everything you need to know about the airlines that introduced lie-flat business class seats to the world.

Lie-flat seats, now industry standard, originated from a single carrier's innovation and drove a competitive arms race that fundamentally reshaped premium cabin profitability.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Golden Dome and the velocity race: Why ground-based optics are the key to mission persistence

While often described as a moonshot for missile defense, the Golden Dome for America mission is in reality something even more challenging: an exercise in disciplined systems engineering under threat. Hypersonic glide vehicles, maneuvering ballistic missiles and complex decoys are compressing...

Ground-based optical systems are becoming critical infrastructure for tracking hypersonic threats, representing a fundamental shift in air defense architecture beyond traditional radar-dependent approaches.

See SpaceNews →

Business Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Bombardier delivers first Global 8000 to NetJets

Bombardier has handed over the first Global 8000 business jet to NetJets, marking the fleet launch customer’s entry…...

NetJets' adoption of the Global 8000 signals market confidence in ultra-long-range cabin technology for fractional ownership, potentially reshaping demand patterns across the business aviation segment.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Up To 17-Hour Nonstop: Emirates' 10 Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Emirates is the world's second-largest long-haul operator, after United Airlines. In 2026, the carrier has an average of 134 daily long-haul passenger departures from its Dubai hub. Over half of the carrier's total flights fall into this category.

Emirates' ultra-long-haul expansion reflects growing hub consolidation around Dubai, requiring aircraft optimized for extended endurance and potential crew fatigue management solutions.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-flight systems offers quicker environmental gains than waiting for next-generation aircraft technology to mature.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

United Airlines transforms devil’s chariot into premium CRJ-450

LOS ANGELES — Before the COVID-19 pandemic upended the airline industry, one-third of all United Airlines’ domestic US departures were on 50-seat regional jets. While that number has fallen to just 10% today, United isn’t resting on its laurels as demand for premium travel products remains high....

United's conversion of 50-seat CRJs to premium-only configurations addresses declining regional jet utilization while capitalizing on elevated yield expectations in post-pandemic travel patterns.

See Runway Girl Network →

Military Mar 26, 2026

South Korea rolls out first mass-production KF-21 Boramae

South Korea has rolled out the first mass-produced KF-21 Boramae fighter jet, completing a major production milestone for…...

South Korea's KF-21 mass production achievement signals regional military aviation capability expansion and potential export competitiveness in the global fighter jet market.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 global launch begins, US delayed again

DJI has kicked off the global rollout of the Avata 360, positioning it as its most immersive consumer drone yet. In many parts of the world, that means orders are either already live or pre-sales have begun. In the US, though, things are playing out a little differently, again. more…...

DJI's continued US market delays suggest regulatory headwinds for consumer drone makers may persist longer than competitors initially anticipated.

See DroneDJ →

Up To 16-Hour Nonstop Flights: Etihad Airways' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Two of the routes are brand-new this year, while another started in 2025. Learn more here!

Ultra-long-range operations require strategic aircraft deployment and crew scheduling optimization to maintain profitability on routes exceeding 16 hours.

Changi Airports International to help develop Phu Quoc as ‘airport destination’

Changi Airports International (CAI) announced it has signed a strategic cooperation agreement with Vietnam’s Sun Group to develop…...

Singapore's airport operator is applying its destination-development model to transform a Vietnamese resort island into a tourism hub centered on airport infrastructure and services.

Military Mar 26, 2026

First Production KF-21 Boramae Rolled Out of Factory in South Korea

The first serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jet has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory in a major milestone for the program. The first of 40 serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jets has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory, marking a major...

South Korea's domestic fighter program transitions from development to full-rate production, reducing dependence on foreign aircraft and strengthening regional aerospace capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to test nuclear electric propulsion with 2028 mission to Mars

NASA’s new exploration strategy includes the rapid development of a nuclear-powered mission to Mars in 2028, leveraging hardware originally built for the lunar Gateway.

Repurposing existing Gateway hardware accelerates NASA's nuclear propulsion timeline while reducing development costs and technical risk for deep space missions.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Airbus tests firefighting system linking drones, aircraft and ground crews

Airbus has demonstrated a concept in which diverse aerial and ground assets share a common data picture during…...

Integrated drone-aircraft-ground coordination could accelerate emergency response by enabling real-time situational awareness across multiple asset types simultaneously.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

A350-900 vs 777-300ER: Who Wins on Range?

For more than two decades, the Boeing 777-300ER has been one of the most recognizable long-haul aircraft in the world. It has carried millions of passengers across continents, linking global hubs like Dubai, New York, Hong Kong, and Los Angeles with remarkable reliability. Airlines have long...

The A350-900's superior fuel efficiency enables comparable range to the 777-300ER while carrying fewer passengers, making it ideal for point-to-point routes but potentially less economical for high-density hub operations.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Striking Differences Between The Boeing 777X & Airbus A350-1000

Find out how the Boeing 777X and Airbus A350-1000 Compete...

The 777X's larger cargo volume and higher seating capacity position it for long-haul freight conversion, while the A350-1000's superior fuel efficiency targets operational cost advantages on dense international routes.
